'''James Batchelor''' (born 9 April 1998) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

He has spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[2017 RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Batchelor's youth team was Crigglestone All Blacks; he was picked up by [[Wakefield Trinity]] via the Wakefield College team.

He is the brother of [[Joe Batchelor]] who plays for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].

==Career==
Batchelor played eight games in 2017 before picking up a hip injury that ended his season.<ref name="Wakefield 10 Warrington 38: Chester left excited at Trinity rookie Batchelor’s debut">{{cite news|url=http://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity/wakefield-10-warrington-38-chester-left-excited-at-trinity-rookie-batchelor-s-debut-1-8068973|title=Wakefield 10 Warrington 38: Chester left excited at Trinity rookie Batchelor's debut|work=The Yorkshire Post|date=2016-08-15|accessdate=2016-09-13}}</ref><ref name="ACADEMY STAR BATCHELOR SIGNS FOUR YEAR DEAL">{{cite web|url=http://www.wakefieldwildcats.co.uk/news/2016/04/academy-star-batchelor-signs-four-year-deal|title=ACADEMY STAR BATCHELOR SIGNS FOUR YEAR DEAL|publisher=Wakefield Wildcats|date=2016-04-01|accessdate=2016-09-13|archive-date=19 September 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160919220421/http://www.wakefieldwildcats.co.uk/news/2016/04/academy-star-batchelor-signs-four-year-deal|url-status=dead}}</ref>
After six years at Wakefield Trinity, Batchelor announced that he had signed a two-year deal to join [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] ahead of the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefields-james-batchelor-confirms-super-league-switch/|title=Wakefield's James Batchelor confirms Super League switch|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=20 August 2022 }}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, Batchelor played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
Batchelor played a total of 22 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=6 October 2023 }}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Batchelor played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, he played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 2025 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 18 September 2025, Batchelor played in Hull Kingston Rovers final game of the season in victory over Warrington which saw the club Lift the League Leaders Shield. <ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo |title=Hull KR see off Wire to win League Leaders' Shield |work=BBC Sport |date=11 September 2025 |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref>
On 11 October, Batchelor played in Hull Kingston Rovers Grand Final victory over Wigan completing the treble <ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/c7v1gd4651vt#Line-ups |title=Super League Grand Final: Hull KR beat Wigan to win title and complete treble |work=BBC Sport |date=11 October 2025 |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref>
On 19 February 2026, Batchelor played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
